positive.prompt: Challenge and refine this rough product idea before saving it
Turn this informal concept into a concise idea draft
Refine this idea before I write a specification or implementation plan
Grill me on this product idea one question at a time until we have a shared understanding

negative.prompt: Review this pull request for correctness and maintainability
Turn this approved specification into an ordered task list
Write an implementation-ready specification for this new feature

evals.prompt: Refine and save this idea: Create a shared internal record of recurring customer complaints so our support team can group duplicates and see which problems are becoming more common. We want to stop relying on scattered chat messages when deciding what to fix.
evals.expected_output: A concise shared-understanding summary and confirmation request after a genuine decision map, without manufacturing objections, asking unnecessary questions, or adding implementation details.
expectations: The response maps the idea's problem framing and causal logic but does not manufacture a weakness
The response does not ask clarification questions or generate alternatives because no material decision remains
The response asks the user to confirm the shared understanding before it drafts or saves anything
The summary does not contain MVP Scope, architecture, source paths, or implementation tasks
No repository exploration or implementation action is attempted

evals.prompt: Refine this idea: I want something that summarizes our meetings with AI.
evals.expected_output: A direct challenge to the solution-first framing followed by one focused, recommendation-backed question that resolves the intended purpose before drafting.
expectations: Exactly one clarification question is asked in the response
The response identifies that the idea commits to AI summaries before establishing the outcome they should serve
The response gives a recommended framing or answer with a short rationale
The question focuses on the outcome or purpose of the meeting summaries
The response does not ask a fixed batch of discovery questions
The response does not inspect a repository, propose architecture, begin implementation, or draft before the decision is reconciled

evals.prompt: Help me refine an idea for a tool that helps open-source maintainers deal with too many issues. I know I want to reduce their triage workload, but I am not sure what kind of help the tool should provide.
evals.expected_output: A small set of materially distinct interpretations used to resolve the idea's genuine directional ambiguity.
expectations: The response offers no more than three distinct directions
Each direction represents a materially different interpretation of the user's intended help
The response asks the user to select or refine the interpretation
The response does not turn the directions into technical designs or implementation plans

evals.prompt: I am inside an existing repository. Refine this idea: let users export their account history so they can keep a personal record before closing an account.
evals.expected_output: A codebase-independent shared-understanding summary and confirmation request that preserves the clear direction without inspecting or citing the repository.
expectations: The response does not read, search, list, or cite repository files
The response does not infer architecture or existing components
The idea is normalized without adding new goals or features
The response asks the user to confirm the shared understanding before it drafts or saves anything
The summary remains outcome-level and contains no technical tasks

evals.prompt: Refine this idea: Require employees to publish daily activity rankings so people feel psychologically safe admitting mistakes.
evals.expected_output: A direct but constructive challenge to the conflict between public comparison and psychological safety, followed by one decision-focused question instead of a draft.
expectations: The response identifies the material tension between public individual comparison and the stated psychological-safety goal
The response explains why the tension could invalidate or redirect the idea
The response offers a concrete better framing or asks whether public comparison is an intentional trade-off
Exactly one decision-focused question is asked
The response does not draft, inspect the repository, or introduce implementation details before the concern is reconciled

evals.prompt: Continue refining this idea with my decision: I understand that public employee rankings may discourage openness, but transparent individual comparison is non-negotiable. Rank employees by how consistently they document mistakes and lessons learned; the goal remains to make mistakes easier to discuss. All material decisions are resolved, and I confirm this is our shared understanding; now draft it.
evals.expected_output: A refined draft after explicit shared-understanding confirmation that preserves the user's chosen direction while explicitly recording the unresolved causal assumption and accepted trade-off.
expectations: The response does not silently remove or weaken the non-negotiable public-ranking mechanism
The draft records the assumption that rankings can increase accountability without preventing honest mistake reporting
The draft records the trade-off between transparent comparison and psychological safety
The response does not pretend the earlier concern has been resolved
The response remains at idea level without architecture or implementation tasks

evals.prompt: Grill me on this idea: I want a service that helps freelancers avoid late payments. Ask one question at a time and do not start a spec until we agree on the idea.
evals.expected_output: A decision-tree grilling response that asks the next highest-leverage idea question, gives a recommendation, and waits without creating a specification or plan.
expectations: Exactly one decision question is asked in the response
The question distinguishes materially different outcomes or directions for avoiding late payments
The response gives a recommended answer or framing and explains why it matters
The response does not send a questionnaire, inspect the repository, draft a specification, create a plan, or implement anything
The response makes clear that the next decision will follow the user's answer rather than a fixed question list
